1.text: Raiders desperate for franchise QB after '25 collapse; Mendoza's pocket poise + arm talent = cleanest QB1 in class.
Same as 7rd mock — Brugler views Mendoza as only true Day-1 starter QB available.
Anonymous scouts: 'He's the only QB with starter traits we'd stake a job on.' Raiders staff reportedly in love post-Pro Day.
Kiper: Mendoza's processing + accuracy over middle separate him; LV has no QB future without him.
McShay: Locked since February — Geno is a bridge, Mendoza is the plan.
Schrager insider intel: 'Raiders brass privately told me this is done.'
Reuter: No-brainer. LV needs QB, Mendoza is QB1 on board.
Same — Reuter consistent across both mocks.
Locker (PFF): Highest-graded QB in class (92.4 PFF grade); LV need is obvious.
Carragher (PFF): Positional-value match — premium position, premium prospect.
Renner: LV rebuild starts under center; Mendoza's traits are the floor they need.
Podell: Team-need fit — QB room is barren post-Geno.
Iyer: Safest QB projection; Raiders can't afford to miss.
Solak (QB-only): Mendoza is his QB1 — 'only true plus-starter.'
Walter WF: Film grade = top prospect overall; fits LV's scheme.
Campbell WF: Pocket-passer archetype Pete Carroll wants.
Davenport (B/R): Fan vote overwhelmingly Mendoza — community consensus.
Band: Combine testing backed up film; LV takes no risk.
Draft Labs: Aggregator median = Mendoza #1 in 100% of mocks.
Mock DB: 87-mock consensus = Mendoza #1 unanimous.

2.text: Brugler: Reese's length + bend fits Aaron Glenn's defense; Jets need EDGE1 opposite Jermaine Johnson.
Same logic — Brugler's two mocks align on Reese.
ESPN Scouts: 'Glenn wants a dominant edge; Reese's upside is highest in class.'
Kiper: Bailey's pass-rush production at TT (17 sacks) edges out Reese for immediate impact.
McShay: Bailey — best pure pass rusher; Jets pick for production over projection.
Schrager: Bailey's motor/get-off wins the coin flip with Reese.
Reuter: Reese — higher ceiling, better fit in Glenn's 3-4 hybrid.
Same as 7rd — Reuter consistent on Reese.
Locker: Reese's PFF pass-rush grade (90.1) is elite; fits analytically.
Carragher: Reese positional value > Bailey.
Renner: Reese's length (35" arms) = Glenn prototype.
Podell: Team need = EDGE; Reese fits scheme.
Iyer: Bailey — production-based pick.
Solak: N/A (QB-only mock).
Walter WF: Bailey — better pure rusher tape.
Campbell WF: Reese — ceiling player.
Davenport: Reese — fan favorite post-combine.
Band: Reese — combine 4.55 40 at 260 lbs stands out.
Draft Labs: Reese plurality across aggregated mocks.
Mock DB: Reese narrowly over Bailey in consensus.

3.text: Brugler: Mauigoa — ARI's OL needs immediate upgrade to protect Kyler.
Brugler 1st: Same — Mauigoa is a plug-and-play LT.
ESPN Scouts: Bailey available here = steal; ARI can't pass on pass rush.
Kiper: Reese — if NYJ takes Bailey, ARI grabs remaining EDGE1.
McShay: NO trades up from #9 to #3 for Reese; ARI slides back gets capital.
Schrager: Reese — ARI's pass rush is the worst weakness on the roster.
Reuter: Mauigoa — OT1 for Kyler protection.
Locker: TEN trades up for Love (RB); ARI gets capital.
Walter WF: DAL trades up to #3 for Reese.

4.text: Brugler: Bailey — EDGE need trumps RB luxury for rebuilding Titans.
ESPN Scouts: Love — 'He's the best skill player, TEN needs weapons for Ward.'
Kiper: Love — best playmaker for young QB Cam Ward.
McShay: Love — Titans want offensive identity around Ward.
Schrager: Love — 'sources indicate TEN is RB-curious at 4.'
Locker: Mauigoa — OL protection for Ward (trade-up scenario).
Walter WF: Love — weapon for Ward.
Band: Love — testing backs up film.

5.text: Brugler: Downs — elite safety, plug-and-play Day 1 starter; NYG secondary in shambles.
ESPN Scouts: Downs — 'no safety has graded this high since Minkah.'
Kiper: Downs — best defensive player available; NYG love his versatility.
McShay: Mauigoa — NYG can't pass on OT after protecting Russell.
Schrager: Styles — Giants pivot to LB after missing on EDGE.
Reuter: Love — NYG need offense; RB1 available.
Renner: Downs — top-10 player on board.

6.text: Brugler: DAL trades up (picks 12+2027 2nd+2027 4th) for Styles — Jerry Jones loves LBs, Styles is elite coverage LB.
Brugler 1st: Same DAL→CLE trade; Styles pairs with Parsons.
ESPN Scouts: Tate — CLE need WR1 badly; 'Tate is Chad Johnson-lite.'
Kiper: DAL→CLE trade for Styles — 'most obvious trade of Round 1.'
McShay: DAL→CLE trade for Styles; CLE gets Parker-era haul.
Schrager: Fano — CLE stays put, OT to protect future QB.
Reuter: Freeling (OT) — CLE goes OL.
Reuter 5rd: Freeling.
Locker: Fano — KC trades up for him (alt scenario).
Carragher: Styles — PFF loves LB coverage grades.
Renner: Fano — OT positional value.
Podell: Fano — team need.
Iyer: Tate — WR1 for next QB.
Solak: N/A.
Walter WF: Tate — WR priority.
Campbell WF: Tate — skill need.
Davenport: Tate — fan vote.
Band: Tate — combine WR1.
Draft Labs: Styles (via DAL trade) — aggregator.
Mock DB: Tate — consensus plurality.

7.text: Brugler: Love — WAS backfield weak; pair with Jayden Daniels.
ESPN Scouts: Tate — Daniels needs WR1.
Kiper: Tate — WR1 for Daniels, obvious fit.
McShay: NYJ trades up to #7 for Tate; WAS slides to #16.

Reuter: Delane — CB1 for depleted Washington secondary.

Walter WF: Styles — Quinn LB fit.

Kiper: Bain Jr. — EDGE need on aging defense.

Reuter: Downs — safety after Mathieu era.

McShay: Downs (S) — alt DB, safety value higher.
Schrager: Delane — 'sources say CIN locked in on CB.'
Reuter: McCoy (CB) — Tennessee CB same value tier.

McShay: Proctor — CLE trade makes OT pick cleaner.

ARI jumps back into Rd1 for developmental QB
CLE uses #24 to move up 4 spots — Brugler's preferred target. Kiper also has CLE→DAL #20 but targets Boston (see row 38).
LAR up for WR — mocked by Reuter twice
KC back into Rd1 for OT
CLE up for OT1 alt scenario
Alt CLE trade — KC instead of DAL
Same CLE→DAL #20 trade structure as row 7, but Kiper targets Boston instead of Concepcion
